scale.description: Geographic scope of the expedition, e.g. 'a single archive-keep', 'a day's march of marsh', 'a mountain range crossed over weeks'.

look.description: The overall place and its mood, in a sentence or two.

locations.description: The distinct places the trials happen at, and how they physically connect.

connectsTo.description: Names of locations this one physically adjoins or leads to.

treasures.description: The significant non-fungible prizes in play (the sought item, a discovered secret), fleshed out.

significance.description: Why it matters to the compact or the world.

cast.description: The animate actors the expedition meets — never the party themselves.

wants.description: What this figure is after — what makes them act on their own ends.

inventory.description: Other significant objects in play, distinct from the treasures.

trials.description: The authored plot — exactly one entry per trial of the expedition, in order.

location.description: Which setting location this trial happens at.

obstacle.description: The significant thing standing in the party's way — a real barrier, not a vague gatekeeper.

whyBarrier.description: Why this obstacle actually blocks the objective or a prize in play.

howResolved.description: How this trial's approach met the obstacle; on a failure, how it fell short and what that cost.

consequence.description: Therefore/but: how this trial's outcome changes the situation going into the next.

cast.description: Names (from cast) who appear in this trial.

wins.description: Name of a treasure/reward obtained at this trial, or null.

closing.description: The payoff: how the expedition ends and the prize is carried home, or lost.
